1560.description: AI is a technology that democratizes wide range of knowledge and still diversity imbalance is reproduced.Globally appr. 22% of AI-relatednpositions are held by women. AI algorithms can contain inherent biases as well - how do we make sure we have the right controls? AI quickly automates administrative roles that are mostly done by women, how they can still be rather winners of the new era than losers? How women can be visible in the fields of AI? What is our responsibility?These are the key topics the accomplished panel members will talk about.

1561.title: Legacy code in the AI era

1562.title: How to weigh AI, human intuition, and engineering?
1562.description: Evelyn van Kelle feat. AVK Podcast

1563.title: How to reinvent yourself in the face of disruption

1564.title: What will happen with juniors now?
1564.description: Emese and Eszpé feat. AVK Podcast

1566.title: Monte Carlo for SaaS: Simulating The Effect Of Product Decisions
1566.description: Most SaaS growth discussions treat acquisition, activation, retention, monetization, and referral as separate metrics. In reality, they interact as a system. A small change in one conversion point can compound through the whole lifecycle, while a seemingly important metric may have little effect on long-term outcomes.In this talk, I will show a Monte Carlo simulator for modeling a B2B SaaS as a state machine.By running many simulations across different SaaS models, we can compare which conversion points have the largest impact on growth, revenue, and customer base size. The goal is not to predict the future exactly, but to reason more clearly about leverage: where improving the product is likely to matter most.The session is aimed at builders, engineers, and product people who want a more systematic way to think about SaaS growth than funnel charts alone.

1567.title: Craft Still Matters
1567.description: As we move toward a world where coding agents do more of the work, what does that actually mean for software craft?Do we still need it? Or can we just go with the Vibes?It turns out that you are still only as fast as your codebase is clean.And one does not simply make an LLM generate code that it can also maintain.Through stories from real work with coding agents, you'll see how craft beats complexity, and why making things extremely small and verifiable is what keeps agent-generated code maintainable, and makes working with agents enjoyable.Software craft is not nostalgia. It is what makes AI-augmented development work.

1568.title: AI in 6G mobile networks
1568.description: Will AI bring a new era to mobile netoworks? Why does the industry call 6G mobile networks AI native and why are we moving in that direction? Do we use AI to build the network or we use the network to enable AI applications? What are the main challenges and what is the current state of the industry answering them?

1569.title: From idea to impact: building successful products in the Agentic AI era
1569.description: What will happen at the Compass AI & Tech Summit?This panel discussion explores the latest trends shaping the future of AI/ML, product development, UX/UI, data, and engineering leadership - and how these disciplines are becoming increasingly interconnected in the AI era.Together with the experts behind Compass, we will dive into the biggest questions each track is currently facing, while uncovering the emerging patterns across the industry.More than just a trend overview, the panel offers a behind-the-scenes look into the themes and discussions attendees can expect at Compass AI & Tech Summit - straight from the people helping shape the agenda itself.

gergely-orosz.name: Gergely Orosz
gergely-orosz.bio:
Gergely writes The Pragmatic Engineer, the #1 technology newsletter on Substack. He was formerly an engineering manager and software engineer at Uber, Skyscanner and Skype/Microsoft. He now researches and writes about software engineering at Big Tech and startups.

Gergely is the author of multiple books, including The Software Engineer’s Guidebook and Building Mobile Apps at Scale. He lives in Amsterdam, the Netherlands.

reuvencohen.name: Reuven Cohen
reuvencohen.bio:
rUv is an AI and Cloud Computing pioneer who coined the term 'cloud computing infrastructure' and helped shape the modern cloud industry. Founded Enomaly, one of the earliest cloud infrastructure companies. A key figure in the emerging field of agentic engineering.

rUv coined the term 'Agentic Engineer' and founded the Agentic Foundation, now active in dozens of cities worldwide. Works with some of the largest agentic AI projects on the planet, collaborating with Fortune 500 companies and government agencies to deploy autonomous agent systems at scale. Built and exited multiple startups across cloud, AI, and developer tools.

michellebrush.name: Michelle Brush
michellebrush.bio:
Michelle Brush has 25 years of software development experience working across embedded software, distributed systems, enterprise platforms, and consumer devices. In her current role at Google, she leads the global teams of SREs that ensure Compute Engine and Persistent Disk are reliable. She is also the author of 2 of the 97 Things Every SRE Should Know. 

Previously, Michelle worked for Cerner Corporation as a software engineer where she built features for clinicians and later was responsible for the data engineering platform for Cerner’s Population Health products. Prior to her time at Cerner, she was the lead engineer for Garmin's automotive routing algorithm and led the map technology department responsible for Garmin’s spatial search, data compression, and shortest pathfinding algorithms. If you’ve ever gotten a route you didn’t like using a Garmin device, it was probably Michelle’s fault.

ryanlopopolo.name: Ryan Lopopolo
ryanlopopolo.bio: Ryan Lopopolo is a software engineer at OpenAI focused on new ways of building software with agents. He writes and speaks about Harness Engineering: the systems, constraints, and feedback loops that make coding agents useful in practice. Before OpenAI, he spent years building developer tools, infrastructure, and large-scale software systems, and he is also the creator of Artichoke Ruby.

dan-north.name: Daniel Terhorst-North
dan-north.bio:
Daniel Terhorst-North invented Behaviour-Driven Development, co-invented the build pipeline, and pioneered many of the Continuous Delivery practices that software teams now take for granted. These were born out of necessity, on real projects, which is why the ideas stuck.

In his thirty-five year career, Daniel has moved from programmer to technical leader to trusted CxO adviser, bridging the worlds of technology and business operations. Since 2012, he has been an independent consultant to organizations from start-ups to household names. A popular keynote speaker at major technology and business conferences worldwide, he has contributed to The RSpec Book and 97 Things Every Programmer Should Know, and blogs at dannorth.net/blog.

veronicalclark.name: Veronica Lynn Clark
veronicalclark.bio: Veronica Lynn Clark is a speaker, author, and facilitator who has spent over two decades helping people get out of their own way and into their lives. She has been featured on The Today Show, appeared on billboards and screens nationally and internationally, and is the author of To Be Aligned — a book so resonant that a conference was named after it. She has guided thousands of people through the work of trusting themselves when everything around them says not to. Veronica doesn’t teach transformation from a distance — she has lived it, rebuilt from it, and chosen it again and again. She is here because it is time.

joseph-pelrine.name: Joseph Pelrine
joseph-pelrine.bio:
A quiet and reserved researcher and practitioner, Joseph Pelrine is considered by cognoscenti to be one of the pioneers and top experts on Agile methods. He has spent almost 40 years defining and refining processes to help some of the world’s most well-known companies improve their ability to satisfy the needs of their customers.

As a psychologist, his focus on people and his experience in applying leading-edge techniques from social complexity and psychology to process optimisation go far beyond the domain of software development and extend to the whole organisation.

Although Joseph Pelrine still occasionally works as a consultant or interim manager, his preference is passing his knowledge on by counselling and advising individuals and organisations, often in the role of a CTO/CIO/CDO “whisperer”. He conducts research in novel applications of psychology to teams and development processes, based his original PhD research in forensic psychology and psycholinguistics.

markrichards3.name: Mark Richards
markrichards3.bio: Mark Richards is an experienced, hands-on software architect involved in the architecture, design, and implementation of microservices architectures and other distributed systems in a variety of technologies. He has been in the software industry since 1983 and has significant experience and expertise in application, integration, and enterprise architecture. Mark is the founder of DeveloperToArchitect.com, a free website devoted to helping developers in the journey to becoming a software architect. In addition to hands-on consulting and training, Mark has authored numerous technical books, including his three latest books Fundamentals of Software Architecture, Software Architecture: The Hard Parts, and Head First Software Architecture that he co-authored with Neal Ford. Mark has spoken at hundreds of conferences and user groups around the world on a variety of enterprise-related technical topics.

iancooper.name: Ian Cooper
iancooper.bio: Coding Architect in London, speaker, tabletop gamer, geek. Talks a lot about TDD, OO and Messaging. Tattooed, pierced, and bearded. The 'guv' of the Brighter Command OSS project

banjo-obayomi.name: Banjo Obayomi
banjo-obayomi.bio: Banjo is a Senior Solutions Architect, where he helps builders ship with generative AI. He focuses on agentic workflows, developer experience, and the practical craft of building reliable AI-powered systems. Outside of work, he runs a local meetup, builds open-source agent tooling, plays JRPGs, and explores whatever events are happening nearby.

chelseatroy.name: Chelsea Troy
chelseatroy.bio: Chelsea Troy leads the machine learning operations team at Mozilla, which makes the Firefox browser. She also teaches at the University of Chicago in the Master's Program in Computer Science.

robert-ranson.name: Robert Ranson
robert-ranson.bio: Robert Ranson is the Founder of HumanRace.ai—the world’s first self-declared agentic engineering firm—and Co-Founder of the Agentics Foundation (with Reuven Cohen), where he works at the leading edge of agentic AI, product strategy, and responsible deployment. A longtime entrepreneur and startup operator who has built multiple companies from the ground up, Robert brings three decades of experience in sales, sales operations, and commercialization to the emerging AI economy. He is the author of The AI Dividend: Preparing for a Post-Labor Economy and has appeared on over 150 podcasts, where he is known for translating complex technological shifts into practical opportunities for founders, executives, and organizations. Alongside his work in AI, Robert is also a traditional artist and sculptor, giving him a distinctive voice on the relationship between human creativity, craftsmanship, and intelligent systems.

martindilger.name: Martin Dilger
martindilger.bio:
Martin has spent most of his career building and evolving distributed systems in the real world, where requirements are incomplete, assumptions are wrong, and certainty is a luxury. Instead of fighting that reality, he’s made it his specialty.

He works with companies and engineering teams to fundamentally improve how they think about software, especially in the age of AI, where change is constant and clarity is rare. Martin helps teams design architectures that are flexible, understandable, and resilient enough to stay maintainable for years, not just releases.

Martin is the author of Understanding Event Sourcing, the first book to present a structured, practical approach to solving the everyday problems software teams actually face.

sander-hoogendoorn.name: Sander Hoogendoorn
sander-hoogendoorn.bio:
Sander Hoogendoorn is an independent dad, traveler, and lifelong software craftsman. With over four decades of hands-on coding experience, he continues to write code daily, demonstrating that real developers never stop learning. Throughout his career, he has navigated the tech world in roles ranging from CTO at companies like iBOOD (currently), ANVA, and Klaverblad to serving as Capgemini's global agile thought leader.

Known for his post-agile mindset and fearless approach to challenging the status quo, Sander helps organizations and teams break free from outdated practices and embrace innovation. He’s not just about writing code—he’s about rewriting the rules.

As an author and dynamic international speaker, Sander shares his insights at conferences worldwide, covering topics such as disruption, culture, life beyond agile, continuous delivery, microteams, software architecture, monads, microservices, and the art of writing elegant code. He champions critical thinking, strategic design, and mindful development to help teams build better software, faster.

Discover Sander—the coding rebel who believes in small steps, big impact, and the mind as the ultimate tool for success.

cat-swetel.name: Cat Swetel
cat-swetel.bio:
As Senior Director of Engineering at Nubank, Cat Swetel is responsible for much of the company's technical backbone. She is the general manager for the Foundation business unit, a critical team that owns from the CICD pipeline forward: cloud and regulatory infrastructure, transactional runtime environments, observability and resilience, storage and databases, and incident response.

An internationally known keynote speaker and a passionate advocate for social change, Cat is frequently invited to share her insights on how technology can create more generative and equitable institutions. With a master's degree in Science and Technology Policy, she brings a unique, interdisciplinary perspective to the intersection of technology, business, and societal well-being.

In her spare time, Cat enjoys cooking, hiking, and helping her family make a huge deal out of even the most minor occasions.

tituswinters.name: Titus Winters
tituswinters.bio: Titus is a Senior Principal Scientist at Adobe, focusing on Developer Experience. He has served on the C++ standards committee, chairing the working group for the design and evolution of the C++ standard library. He has also served on the ACM/IEEE/AAAI CS2023 steering committee, helping set curriculum requirements for Computer Science undergraduate degrees, focusing on the requirements for software engineering. Titus was a thought leader at Google for many years, focusing on C++, software engineering practice, technical debt, and culture. He is the lead author for the book Software Engineering at Google. (O'Reilly, 2020).

granicz.name: Adam Granicz
granicz.bio: Adam Granicz (@granicz) is a 14x F#/.NET MVP and the coauthor of five F# books, key community member and evangelist, a regular speaker at developer conferences and workshops, and the Founder of IntelliFactory, the F# company specializing in training, consulting, and developing functional, reactive web, cloud and AI applications.

kevlin.name: Kevlin Henney
kevlin.bio: Kevlin Henney is a independent consultant, trainer, technologist, writer and renowned keynote speaker known for his work helping organisations, teams and individuals with their software development practices, processes and architectures. He is a contributor to the Modern Software Engineering YouTube channel, co-author of two volumes in the Pattern-Oriented Software Architecture series, editor of 97 Things Every Programmer Should Know, co-editor of 97 Things Every Java Programmer Should Know, and has written for many other publications, including O'Reilly Radar and The Register.

marianhartman.name: Marian Hartman
marianhartman.bio:
Marian Hartman helps teams and leaders create change that actually sticks.

She has 17 years of experience in teaching adults in the academic and corporate worlds on how to adopt new behaviours and use new skills to adjust their way of doing things.

kenny-baas-schwegler.name: Kenny (Baas) Schwegler
kenny-baas-schwegler.bio:
I believe in collaborative software design where ‘every voice shapes the software’. Leveraging a domain-driven design approach with Team Topologies, I facilitate clearer communication between stakeholders and software creators by collaborative modelling and deep democracy, decoding complexities, resolving conflicts and ensuring software remains agile to business demands.

As a lead software architect for DHL BeNeLux and independent consultant and trainer, I specialise in technical leadership, software architecture, and sociotechnical system design. I work with organisations and teams to design and build sustainable and resilient software architecture.

evelyn-van-kelle.name: Evelyn van Kelle
evelyn-van-kelle.bio: Evelyn van Kelle is a change management consultant working in technical environments, where she helps organizations design and sustain sociotechnical systems. With her experience, she focuses on how human behavior and social dynamics shape the way technology is built and adopted. Evelyn is a strategic thinker who values curiosity and questions over quick answers. She supports organizations in navigating change and creating environments where desired behaviors can thrive. She is also the co-author of Collaborative Software Design (Manning Publications). Next to this, she loves food, cooking, and everything that comes with that.

matthewclarkuk.name: Matthew Clark
matthewclarkuk.bio: Matthew Clark is Head of Architecture for the BBC’s online products. Over 15 years at the BBC, he has worked on hundreds of projects - from delivering coverage of Olympic Games around the world to helping get BBC iPlayer running on the International Space Station. His focus is technical strategy: how emerging technologies can be applied pragmatically to solve real problems, move faster, and create distinctive digital experiences.

hillel-wayne.name: Hillel Wayne
hillel-wayne.bio: Hillel is the software engineer specializing in formal methods, distributed systems, and software history. He is the author of Logic for Programmers, Practical TLA+ and the Crossover Project. He has done formal verification and training for clients like NASA, Meta, Giesecke+Devrient, McKinsey & Company, Siemens AG, and Western Digital. In his free time, he juggles and makes chocolate. He has a blog at hillelwayne.com and a https://buttondown.email/hillelwayne/ weekly newsletter at. He currently works for Antithesis.

simonrohrer.name: Simon Rohrer
simonrohrer.bio:
Simon Rohrer, Head of Enterprise Architecture and Ways of Working at Saxo Bank has spent his career advocating for modern ways of working, with roles in hands on software engineering, agile coaching with a focus on technical excellence, and senior roles in enterprise architecture and strategy.

He's a co-author of Jonathan Smart's IT Revolution Press book Sooner Safer Happier, and working on a chapter on organising for outcomes for the forthcoming follow-up book.

artie-shevchenko.name: Artie Shevchenko
artie-shevchenko.bio:
Artie Shevchenko is the author of Code Health Guardian. A former Google software engineer and startup founder, he is currently a software engineer at Canva and a lecturer at ITMO University.

Artie draws on broad industry experience spanning over 15 years to inform his pragmatic approach to code health. His career includes leadership and technical roles—as a CEO, engineering manager, and software engineer—within environments ranging from tech giants like Google and Canva to medium-sized companies and agile startups.

neal-ford.name: Neal Ford
neal-ford.bio: Neal is an independent software consultant, best-selling author, and international speaker. He is an internationally recognized expert on software development and delivery, especially in the intersection of agile engineering techniques and software architecture. Neal has authored magazine articles, nine books (and counting), dozens of video presentations, and spoken at hundreds of developers conferences worldwide. His topics include software architecture, continuous delivery, functional programming, cutting edge software engineering, and includes a business-focused book and video on improving technical presentations. He is an innovator in building concrete verification mechanisms for software architecture, for humans or agents. Check out his web site at nealford.com.

matthewmaisel.name: Matthew Maisel
matthewmaisel.bio: Matt Maisel is the co-founder and CTO of Sondera, an AI security startup building evaluation and runtime harnesses for trustworthy AI. For over 15 years, he has built ML-powered security products at companies such as Cylance, Obsidian Security, and NetRise. Currently, he focuses on building, breaking, and securing agentic systems. He has presented at Unprompted, BSides, and the DEF CON AI Village, and has led training sessions at Black Hat USA. He holds an M.S. in Computer Science from Johns Hopkins University.

xinyao.name: Xin Yao
xinyao.bio:
Xin Yao is an independent consultant, trainer, and sociotechnical architect based in Copenhagen. She supports organizations across Europe in navigating complex design, architecture and modernization challenges. Her work blends Domain-Driven Design, collaborative modeling, sociotechnical thinking, psychological safety, and conversational leadership.

Drawing on her background as a chief architect and years of facilitating large-scale change, Xin brings a structured yet deeply human approach to connecting business, technology, and teams.

She helps organizations integrate relational and conversational practices into architecture, strategy, product design, and cross-team collaboration—enabling better decisions, clearer models, and more joyful teamwork.

Xin regularly teaches and speaks at events such as GOTO, DDD Europe, KanDDDinsky, Explore DDD, OOP, JAX, Øredev, CoMoCamp, FlowCon, and Craft.

wesreisz.name: Wesley Reisz
wesreisz.bio:
With over 25 years of delivering and architecting sociotechnical systems, Wesley Reisz has led the technical delivery of multi-million dollar software projects, chaired numerous software conferences across North America and the United Kingdom, created a highly respected podcast, and spent over a decade teaching 400-level software architecture/programming courses as an adjunct professor. These experiences have given him deep expertise in software architecture, cloud-native engineering, team topologies, and platform thinking (alongside a broad knowledge of different software domains).

Wes is a Technical Principal at Thoughtworks, where he specializes in leading technical teams that reduce complexity in software through systems thinking, application modernization, platform engineering, and AI-First Software Delivery. Embodying the concept of a T-shaped engineer (blending broad expertise across a wide range of software domains with deep technical knowledge of the cloud-native ecosystem), Wes strongly believes in the transformative power of sharing knowledge through speaking, teaching, and continuous learning.

viktor-micsko.name: Viktor Micskó
viktor-micsko.bio: Viktor Micskó is a staff software engineer at SEON, where he is part of the Performance Engineering team responsible for the architectural evolution described in this talk. He has hands-on experience with each phase of this journey, from implementing the ClickHouse integration to driving the redesign and migration of the scoring system on YugabyteDB.

gabriella-matyas-kollar.name: Gabriella Mátyás-Kollár
gabriella-matyas-kollar.bio:
Shiwaforce.com’s AI development team is led by Gabriella Mátyás-Kollár, an economist and AI innovation expert helping enterprises adopt AI through strategy, training, consulting, and solution development. She holds an Executive MBA from INSEAD and has international experience across finance and technology, including senior leadership roles at Morgan Stanley, Erste, Allianz, and AEGON.

She specializes in executive AI strategy, digital transformation, and innovation, with a strong focus on responsible AI, organizational culture change, and AI’s societal impact. Gabriella is a frequent speaker at conferences and professional programs, and founder of Women & AI Hungary, supporting greater female participation in AI and STEM fields.

katalin-hornyik.name: Katalin Hornyik
katalin-hornyik.bio:
I originally aspired to become a chemist, but an unexpected success in an IT competition redirected my path toward technology. I later earned two master’s degrees from ELTE: an MSc in Informatics (Program Designer Mathematician) and an MSc in Mathematics and Chemistry Education.

Although I never pursued teaching professionally, my academic background has shaped the structured, analytical approach I bring to my work. Over the course of my career, I have held a wide range of roles in IT, including software developer (from junior to lead), line manager, release manager, software architect, and now Software Development Manager.

My core technical strengths lie in Java backend development and relational database systems across multiple platforms. I am particularly passionate about data, system design, and software architecture, and I enjoy building solutions that balance technical quality with long‑term sustainability.

Outside of work, I am a dedicated wife and mother of three children, aged 4 to 19. I work part‑time and strongly advocate for healthy work‑life balance, believing that people perform at their best when both their professional and personal lives are supported.

rozalia-miklos.name: Rozália Miklós
rozalia-miklos.bio: Rozália Miklós is Managing Director at MSCI, where she leads the global Financial Engineering team within the Analytics business and oversees MSCI's 500+ person Budapest office. She started her career at K&H Bank before joining MSCI in 2013, where she has held a variety of leadership roles across Technology, Product, and Research teams. She is an advisory board member of the WILL Foundation for Women Leaders, and an active mentor in the HBLF X Mentor Program, the Egyenlítő Foundation, and the Menedzserszövetség mentoring programs. In 2024 she received the "Manager of the Year for Business Culture" award from Menedzserszövetség and KPMG, and the Mentor Oscar award.

jozsef-holderith.name: József Holderith
jozsef-holderith.bio:
A representative of the software partner division at IBM Hungary, with more than 20 years of enterprise sales experience. For over a decade, he represented IBM’s Data and AI business unit, while throughout his career he has also gained extensive experience at smaller IT startups as well as major global IT vendors, with a particular focus on the software and telecommunications sectors.

His commitment to knowledge sharing is reflected in the fact that he is the co-founder and host of the mAI podcast, where he engages in discussions with Hungarian experts and presents the latest trends, challenges, and innovations in the world of AI and data analytics through real-life case studies.

emese-pogacsas.name: Emese Pogácsás
emese-pogacsas.bio:
Emese Pogácsás is a leadership coach and former VP of Engineering, working with tech leaders navigating complexity, organizational change, and the evolving expectations of software engineering.

With a background in software engineering and years spent building and leading engineering organizations, she brings both technical and leadership perspectives to conversations about the future of the craft, AI, and human systems.

Today, her work focuses on helping leaders make better decisions through clearer thinking, communication, and awareness — especially in fast-changing technology environments. Check out her work at Namaste & Cabernet.

adam-kovacs.name: Ádám Kovács
adam-kovacs.bio:
Ádám is the Co-founder and Chief AI Strategist at the AI Enablement Academy, partnering with startups and scaleups, VCs, accelerators, and enterprise-scale organizations on the operational layer of AI adoption: agentic workflows, governance, and system design that enable it to scale across functions. He is also a cofounding member of the Agentics Foundation, trained in ISO 56001 innovation management, and the Executive Director of OPEN Talent Society, a Seattle-based non-profit exploring the future of work.

He was a founding member of the AWS Talent Intelligence service, scaling it into a global program serving thousands of internal stakeholders and impacting multiple tens of thousands of hires. While running people ops at scale, he’ve found that initiatives in large organizations succeed or stall not on the strength of the model but on what sits on top and underneath it, on the data maturity, structured process, and governance that let a new system actually scale.

klara-hermesz.name: Klara Hermesz
klara-hermesz.bio:
Klara designs the human side of technology adoption at a global scale. 

She is the co-founder and Chief Learning Strategist at the AI Enablement Academy, partnering with enterprise-scale organizations across the full arc of AI adoption: strategy, change management, human and organizational enablement, and the application of AI in actual business and operational workflows. She also runs RedRebel Learning, her executive coaching consultancy, working with leaders 1:1, giving them room to learn AI at the altitude they operate at without already knowing the answers.

At Meta, she built the first Metaverse education strategy for a 3,000-person global people organization. At Uber, she redesigned the global mentoring and coaching program for 50,000+ employees. 

The red thread she kept seeing across both: new technology lands and whether it thrives or dies in an organization depends less on the tool than on the system of enablement around it, on how people are taught, supported, and brought along through the change.

michael-feathers.name: Michael Feathers
michael-feathers.bio: Michael Feathers is the Chief Architect of Globant and founder and Director of R7K Research & Conveyance, a company specializing in software and organization design. Over the years, Michael has spent a great deal of time helping teams after design over time in code bases. Michael is also the author of the book Working Effectively with Legacy Code (Prentice Hall, 2004).

nickolas-means.name: Nickolas Means
nickolas-means.bio: Nickolas Means loves nothing more than a story of engineering triumph (except maybe a story of engineering disaster). When he's not stuck in a Wikipedia loop reading about plane crashes, he leads the engineering team at Sym, helping create the building blocks engineering teams need to build delightful security and compliance workflows. He works remotely from Austin, TX, and spends his spare time hanging out with his wife and kids, going for runs, and trying to brew the perfect cup of coffee.

ian-thomas.name: Ian Thomas
ian-thomas.bio:
Ian is a Software Engineer at Meta currently working as a Privacy Area Tech Lead for Choice in the User Risk team. Previously, he helped lead development of Horizon Worlds, Horizon Workrooms and Workplace, after spending nearly a decade building sports betting systems for Sky Bet and PokerStars.

Ian is interested in combining technology with user experience design, focusing on languages, platforms and tools to build products that customers interact with directly. He is also passionate about engineering excellence and developer experience; working to drive up quality and enjoyment for software teams.

arlobelshee.name: Arlo Belshee
arlobelshee.bio:
Arlo Belshee is CTO of Eolus.ai (https://eolus.ai) and Distinguished Engineer in Minions! — the ecosystem organization for agentic orchestration in brownfield code.

At Eolus.ai, he led the development of an AI agent for pre-construction estimation on large commercial projects. Eolus brings advanced parametric estimation tools to AI so building owners can iterate on a project idea long before an architect or GC is in the room.

He has spent two decades working in some of the ugliest codebases on the planet — up to 65 million lines, teams of up to 1,000 — developing practices that eliminate bugs without requiring people to try harder. His work at Microsoft reduced bugs per release by over 99%. His practices (#ZeroBugs, Risk-aware Commit Notation, Naming as a Process, Safeguarding) share a common premise: when someone makes a mistake, the environment is the problem, not the person.

That premise now powers his AI agent work. Minions is an orchestration system built on the same design principle. Don't ask the agent to be more careful; design its world so careless behavior still succeeds. He's been applying that in anger on real brownfield codebases, and this talk is the distillation of what he's learned.

Arlo is the founder of Deep Roots (https://digdeeproots.com), which offers certification training in technical practices for legacy code. He writes at Refactoring with Depth (https://digdeeproots.substack.com) on Substack.

peter-szasz.name: Péter Szász
peter-szasz.bio: Péter is a father, cyclist, backgammon player, cookbook collector, and has led engineering teams for 25+ years across web development, online media, and security businesses. At Dashlane, Gawker Media, Graphisoft, and other companies, he supported managers, built high-performing engineering teams, and delivered meaningful products while navigating complex transitions. He mentors Engineering Leaders and publishes a weekly newsletter addressing real-world Engineering Management challenges on peterszasz.com.

gojko.name: Gojko Adzic
gojko.bio: Author of Lizard Optimization, Impact Mapping, Specification by Example and a few more books… Working on Narakeet and Votito.

aaronerickson.name: Aaron Erickson
aaronerickson.bio:
Aaron Erickson leads NemoClaw Engineering at NVIDIA, where he and his team are building systems for reliable agentic software. His current work focuses on making AI agents practical in real engineering environments through sandboxed execution, policy controls, inference routing, lifecycle management, and integration with developer workflows.

Aaron has spent his career building software at the intersection of platforms, engineering leadership, and applied AI. Before NVIDIA, he held engineering leadership roles at ThoughtWorks and New Relic, and was CEO and co-founder of Orgspace, an early generative-AI startup for organizational design. He is also the author of “The Nomadic Developer” and “Professional F# 2.0.”

His current focus is turning powerful AI agents from clever demos into systems that engineers can operate, evaluate, and trust.

randy-shoup.name: Randy Shoup
randy-shoup.bio:
Randy has spent more than three decades building distributed systems and high performing teams, and has worked as a senior technology leader at eBay, Google, Stitch Fix, and WeWork. He coaches CTOs, advises companies, and generally makes a nuisance of himself wherever possible. He talks a lot -- sometimes at conferences about software -- and is interested in the nexus of culture, technology, and organization.

He is currently SVP Engineering at Thrive Market.

ashi-krishnan.name: Ashi Krishnan
ashi-krishnan.bio: ashi (she/they) is a writer and technologist who has been working with machines since before they could talk. she thinks they're pretty interesting, but thinks people are far moreso.

sarah-wells.name: Sarah Wells
sarah-wells.bio:
Sarah is a technology leader, consultant and conference speaker with a focus on engineering effectiveness, microservices, incident management, platform engineering, optimising for flow and technical strategy. She has over 20 years’ experience as a developer, principal engineer and tech director across product, platform, SRE and DevOps teams.

She spent over a decade working at the Financial Times, as it transformed from 12 releases a year to more than 20,000, embracing autonomous empowered teams and adopting microservices, DevOps, containers and platform engineering.

She is the author of the O’Reilly book Enabling Microservice Success: Managing Technical, Organizational and Cultural Challenges.

andraspalffy.name: Andras Palffy
andraspalffy.bio:
Andras Palffy received an M.Sc. degree in computer science engineering from Pazmany Peter Catholic University, Hungary, in 2016, and another M.Sc. degree in digital signal and image processing from Cranfield University, U.K., in 2015.

From 2013 to 2017, he was an algorithm researcher at Eutecus, a US based startup acquired by Verizon, developing computer vision algorithms for traffic monitoring and ADAS applications.

He obtained his Ph.D. degree in 2022 at Delft University of Technology, Netherlands, focusing on radar based road user detection for automated driving. He is the first author of the "View of Delft" dataset, which is one of the most widely used automotive dataset including 4D radar data, used by 800+ researchers around the world.

In 2022 he co-founded Perciv AI, a machine perception startup developing AI-driven, next generation machine perception for radars.

abbybangser.name: Abby Bangser
abbybangser.bio:
Abby is a Principal Engineer at Syntasso delivering Kratix, the execution later for your internal platforms. Her keen interest in supporting internal development comes from over a decade of experience in consulting and product delivery roles across platform, site reliability, and quality engineering.

Abby is a CNCF Ambassador, Team Topologies Advocate, emeritus lead of the CNCF Platforms Working Group, and co-host of the #CoffeeOps London meetup.

dragan-spiridonov.name: Dragan Spiridonov
dragan-spiridonov.bio:
Dragan Spiridonov brings 30 years of IT experience—from computer repair and sysadmin in 1996 to establishing and leading QA/QE functions for the past 12 years. After 8 years at Alchemy, building QA/QE from the ground up, he founded Quantum Quality Engineering, a Serbian consultancy that bridges classical quality engineering with agentic AI approaches.

A practitioner of context-driven testing, Holistic Testing, and Rapid Software Testing, Dragan adapted the PACT Framework (Proactive, Autonomous, Collaborative, Targeted) from Agentic Engineering and created the Agentic QE framework to help teams with guides on how to approach integrating QE agents into quality workflows. He has built three open-source Agentic testing platforms and shares his experiences in his blog, The Quality Forge.

As an ambassador for the Agentic Foundation, he's actively building a community focused on practical AI adoption in quality engineering and founding the Serbian chapter. His recent work includes publishing on AI's impact on QA leadership and speaking at international conferences about lessons learned from building testing platforms with agents.

Dragan's philosophy: quality must be built in, not tested in—and best practices are always contextual.

adam-litter.name: Adam Litter
adam-litter.bio: At DATAPAO, I work as a Machine Learning & Data Engineering Consultant, and I also support sales engineering activities as part of the sales engineering team. Over the past few years, I’ve had the opportunity to work with dozens of international clients on both short- and long-term projects, which has given me a comprehensive understanding of how different organizations are able to adopt AI technologies, as well as the organizational and technical challenges they encounter in practice.

florincoros.name: Florin Coros
florincoros.bio:
Florin is an accomplished Software Solution Architect and an engaging Technical Trainer with two decades of hands-on experience in complex software development.

As the founder of Code Design, he is at the forefront of restoring a focus on robust software design within the industry and ensuring predictability in software development projects. Florin's courses contain the hard learned lessons he gained in his work. They empower professionals to develop the habits which will elevate their design skills.

An IDesign trained architect, Florin is dedicated to steering projects towards success across multiple companies. He achieves this by assuming pivotal roles such as Software Architect or Solution Architect, where his active leading has been crucial in the successful realisation of many projects.

duncanjbrown.name: Duncan Brown
duncanjbrown.bio: Duncan Brown is CTO for Digital Prevention Services at NHS England. Before working for the NHS he was Head of Software Engineering at the British Government's Incubator for AI.

kiefmorris.name: Kief Morris
kiefmorris.bio:
Kief Morris (he/him) has been helping technology organizations in all sorts of domains to improve their use of automation to streamline delivery of digital services since the dot-com days.

He has written three editions of the book Infrastructure as Code, published by O’Reilly, and works with clients and partners globally as a Distinguished Engineer at Thoughtworks.

Originally from Tennessee, Kief has lived in London for almost 30 years.

gregor-riegler.name: Gregor Riegler
gregor-riegler.bio:
Gregor has spent the better part of two decades shipping software and helping teams get better at the craft of it.
He's also a member of the Samman Technical Coaching Society, and a maintainer of mob.sh.
These days he is obsessed with teaching coding agents how to apply software craft.

adamtornhill.name: Adam Tornhill
adamtornhill.bio: Adam Tornhill is a programmer who combines degrees in engineering and psychology. He's the founder of CodeScene, where he designs tools for software analysis. He's also the author of the best-selling Your Code as a Crime Scene, and three more technical books. Adam is an award-winning software researcher, focusing on code quality and AI. His other interests include music, retro computers, and martial arts.

jacob-kahn.name: Jacob Kahn
jacob-kahn.bio: Jacob is a research manager and technical leader at FAIR, Meta AI and is on the computer science faculty at the University of Pennsylvania. His current research focus areas are code generation and reasoning where he leads teams across California, New York, and Paris. Jacob previously worked in multi-modal generative models, speech recognition; he consistency works at the intersections of machine learning, distributed systems, and performance engineering.

llewellynfalco.name: Llewellyn Falco
llewellynfalco.bio: Llewellyn Falco learned to jump horses in the 7th grade while living in France. Back in states, while studying drafting in high school, he started fire eating, sleight of hand magic, and once rode a unicycle 6 miles. After learning to juggle torches, he joined a acrobatics group in college where he specialized on the trampoline and walking a slack rope. He can calculate the cube root of any perfect cube under 1,000,000 in his head, as well as pick a standard lock. He can rollerblade down a flight of stairs, backwards. Later, he has learned to play the doumbek (a type of drum), to accompany a belly dancing girlfriend. Llewellyn studied Tai Chi for 2 years, can throw a knife at 20 feet, and a playing card at 50. He has taught swing dancing, and loves to salsa. He is also an accomplished speed chess player. He has been scuba diving over 20 times, become a guitarhero, and broke his personal record of paddle balling over 200 times. Llewellyn attributes his success to the large amount of caffeine he has consumed, and enjoys computer programming in his spare time.

lada-kesseler.name: Lada Kesseler
lada-kesseler.bio: Lada is a seasoned practitioner of extreme programming, Test-Driven Development, and Domain-Driven Design who transforms complex legacy systems into maintainable architectures through incremental improvement and continuous refactoring. Her approach pairs deep technical expertise with genuine empathy for both end users and fellow developers, creating resilient systems that truly serve their users. Over the past year and a half, she's been exploring how to bring the same rigor and craft to AI-assisted development, figuring out what it takes to build reliable software when the tools themselves are unreliable.

marton-szabo.name: Márton Szabó
marton-szabo.bio: Márton is a Talent Partner at The Tech Talent Co., an official Sequoia recruiting partner and specialist technical recruiting consultancy built by ex-in-house recruiters from Palantir, Meta, and Alibaba. He partners with some of Europe's fastest-scaling AI companies - including Lovable (Europe's fastest-growing AI company), Synthesia (UK's most valuable AI company), and Profound (unicorn in under 16 months) - as well as companies like Framer, Attio, and Citadel, sourcing senior engineers across AI/ML, platform, FDE, and product roles. Working across the hiring pipelines of multiple high-growth companies simultaneously gives him a cross-company lens on what elite AI teams actually optimise for when building engineering organisations, and what separates the top 1% from the rest.

janos-csorvasi.name: János Csorvási
janos-csorvasi.bio: János is a Software Engineer at Tesco Technology, working on mission critical systems that power the company’s global technical ecosystem and building platforms for his fellow engineers. With a decade of experience in platform engineering and leading DevOps transformations at scale, he is passionate about distributed systems, telemetry, and enabling teams to move fast without worrying about infrastructure.

julia-raksimowicz.name: Julia Raksimowicz
julia-raksimowicz.bio:
Julia is a Software Engineer at Tesco Technology with a decade of experience

in distributed systems engineering. She works on Inter-Service Communication —

the mission-critical platform that routes all traffic across Tesco's global estate. Her

focus is on solving the hard problems that make Tier 0 traffic infrastructure

observable, operable, and accessible — so that product engineering teams can

reason about their systems without needing to understand the control plane beneath

them.

benedek-kovacs.name: Benedek Kovács
benedek-kovacs.bio:
Benedek Kovács joined Ericsson in 2005 and worked as a researcher, tester, software developer, and lead network performance specialist for VoLTE (4G voice) and VoNR (5G voice). Today, he is an edge computing expert at Ericsson and technical lead for 6G proof of concepts, focusing especially on edge AI for 6G.

In his career, besides leading the technology and innovation team in Budapest, he has worked at Ericsson’s Spain, Sweden, and Silicon Valley offices, leading and coordinating global engineering teams and projects.

He is an author of several 4G, 5G, and 6G patents, holds an M.Sc. in information engineering and a Ph.D. in mathematics from the Budapest University of Technology and Economics (BME) in Hungary.

balazs-nemethi.name: Balázs Némethi
balazs-nemethi.bio: Balázs Nemethi is a Hungarian technologist and entrepreneur working at the intersection of internet infrastructure, identity, and AI. He is the founder of the Agent Community at agentcommunity.org, a community-backed effort to establish .agent as a new top-level domain and identity layer for AI agents, and the author of AID, a DNS-first standard for agent identity and discovery. Previously, he scaled the Decentralized Identity Foundation from roughly 50 to more than 300 organizations, founded Tqanu, a financial-inclusion company serving refugees in Germany, and built web3 compliance infrastructure at Veri Labs, where he is the inventor on a granted U.S. patent for programmable access control on blockchain assets.
